md: If you went left to right, you got $20$ (computing $2 + 3 = 5$, then $\times 4 = 20$). If you multiplied first, you got $14$ (computing $3 \times 4 = 12$, then $2 + 12 = 14$).
**Math has agreed on a single order**, so every expression has exactly one correct value. That order is called **PEMDAS**.
**M**ultiplication and **D**ivision (left to right, *not* M before D)
**A**ddition and **S**ubtraction (left to right, *not* A before S)
Some places use **BODMAS** (brackets, orders, division/multiplication, addition/subtraction). Same idea, different name.

blocks.title: The trap people fall into
blocks.body: PEMDAS is **not** P → E → M → D → A → S in strict order. **M and D are tied** — do them left to right. Same for **A and S**. So $8 \div 2 \times 4$ is $16$, not $1$ — division and multiplication, evaluated left to right.

blocks.title: Tip — show your work
blocks.body: Rewrite the expression after each step, replacing the part you computed. It looks slow but it makes mistakes obvious. Computers and calculators do exactly this internally.

blocks.title: Recap
blocks.body: **P → E → M/D → A/S**, with M/D and A/S evaluated left to right. Parentheses always come first. When in doubt, add parentheses to make your intent clear.
